generated_oml:
// C1: Environment - ManufacturingSystem
instance ManufacturingSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment containing the industrial robot system with motor and gearboxes for manufacturing automation. Characterized by one-way data flow from physical components to digital representation for friction loss detection."
    base:contains PhysicalRobotSystem, MotorComponent, GearboxComponent1, GearboxComponent2
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary drive motor using Permanent Magnet Synchronous technology connected via cycloidal gear transmission system for torque amplification in the manufacturing robot."
]

instance SiemensSinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial CNC controller (Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl) providing real-time actuation control for the robot system with networked communication capabilities via Ethernet/IP or MQTT."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance DrivePositionEncoder1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder (0.06mm path repeatability, 0.1mm positional accuracy) mounted on the first drive axis for kinematic tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DrivePositionData1
]

instance DrivePositionEncoder2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder (0.06mm path repeatability, 0.1mm positional accuracy) mounted on the second drive axis for kinematic tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DrivePositionData2
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance DrivePositionData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DrivePositionEncoder1
]

instance DrivePositionData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DrivePositionEncoder2
]

// C5: Components
instance PhysicalRobotSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Industrial robot (MABI MAX 100 type) with mechanical subsystem consisting of motor and two-stage gear transmission for manufacturing automation tasks."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingSystemEnvironment
]

instance MotorComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical subsystem component containing the Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor and its associated drive electronics."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingSystemEnvironment
]

instance GearboxComponent1 : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First-stage cycloidal gearbox providing primary torque amplification for the robot's primary axis."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingSystemEnvironment
]

instance GearboxComponent2 : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Second-stage gearbox providing additional torque reduction for precise motion control in the manufacturing system."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingSystemEnvironment
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 Internal Block Diagram representing the functional/logical architecture of the robot system including component-connector relationships between motor, gearboxes, and controller."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model specified using MontiArc Automaton language representing state transitions and operational logic for the manufacturing robot system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcToolEnabler
]

instance SimulinkDynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model representing detailed dynamic behavior of the mechanical system including motor control and gearbox interactions for performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolEnabler
]

instance TimeSeriesSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series data collected from MQTT streams representing raw sensor readings from position encoders and derived metrics for system behavior analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DrivePositionData1, DrivePositionData2
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance FrictionLossMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived metrics representing calculated friction loss values between mechanical components (motor-gearbox interfaces) based on sensor data correlations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TimeSeriesSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalyticsEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool (e.g., MagicDraw, Cameo) used for creating and managing Internal Block Diagrams and state machines representing the system architecture."
    DTDFVocab:enables ArchitectureModelingService
]

instance ArchitectureDescriptionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ACME/AADL architecture description language framework enabling component-connector composition and interface specification between physical components."
    DTDFVocab:enables ArchitectureAnalysisService
]

instance MontiArcToolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc toolchain supporting MontiArc Automaton language for formal behavioral specification and verification of system operations."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkToolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for detailed dynamic system simulation including control system design and real-time processing capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance ControlSystemEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded control system implementation using C++/Java for real-time processing and integration between modeling tools and physical system."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAlgorithmService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database system (e.g., InfluxDB) for storing and processing aggregated sensor data streams from MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard visualization framework (e.g., Grafana) for presenting aggregated data traces and derived metrics to engineering stakeholders."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance AnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven analytics engine using SysML-based models and state machines to infer system behavior patterns from sensor data correlations."
    DTDFVocab:enables InsightGenerationService
]

instance DecisionSupportEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Semantic data linking framework enabling relation detection between physical and digital states for friction loss detection and diagnostic insights."
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService
]

// C6: Services
instance ArchitectureModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for creating and maintaining SysML Internal Block Diagrams representing the component-connector architecture of the manufacturing robot system."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureModelInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for specifying and verifying behavioral models using MontiArc Automaton language for system operations."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralModelInsight
]

instance ArchitectureAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for analyzing component-connector relationships using ACME/AADL architecture description languages."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureAnalysisInsight
]

instance BehavioralAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for analyzing state transitions and operational logic using MontiArc Automaton models."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralAnalysisInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for detailed dynamic simulation of the mechanical system using Simulink models including motor control and gearbox interactions."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationResultsInsight
]

instance ControlAlgorithmService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing real-time control algorithms in C++/Java for processing sensor data and generating control commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAlgorithmInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for collecting and aggregating time-series sensor data from MQTT streams into structured digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides AggregatedDataInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing dashboard-based visualization of aggregated data traces and derived metrics for stakeholder interpretation."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance InsightGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating insights from time-series sensor data and model-sensor correlations for friction loss detection and system behavior analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossInsight, SystemBehaviorInsight
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting decision-making through semantic data linking and visualization of model-sensor relationships for diagnostic purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides DecisionSupportInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ArchitectureModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight representing the SysML Internal Block Diagram architecture showing component-connector relationships in the manufacturing robot system."
]

instance BehavioralModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight representing the MontiArc Automaton behavioral model showing state transitions and operational logic for the robot system."
]

instance ArchitectureAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from ACME/AADL architecture analysis showing component interfaces and composition hierarchies."
]

instance BehavioralAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from MontiArc Automaton analysis showing verified state transition patterns and operational behaviors."
]

instance SimulationResultsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from Simulink simulation results showing dynamic behavior of the mechanical system including motor control and gearbox interactions."
]

instance ControlAlgorithmInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from real-time control algorithm processing showing processed sensor data and generated control commands."
]

instance AggregatedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight representing aggregated time-series data from MQTT streams showing raw sensor readings and derived metrics."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from dashboard visualizations showing relational analysis of system components and derived metrics for stakeholder interpretation."
]

instance FrictionLossInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight representing calculated friction loss values between mechanical components (motor-gearbox interfaces) based on sensor data correlations."
]

instance SystemBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing inferred system behavior patterns from model-sensor correlations enabling predictive understanding of manufacturing robot performance."
]

instance DecisionSupportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight supporting engineering decisions through semantic data linking and visualization of model-sensor relationships for friction loss detection."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's temporal aspects are governed by the need for synchronized data flows between physical and digital components, though explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed in the provided documents. The context mentions that Digital Shadows (a related concept) involve one-way automated data flows, implying that Digital Twin systems may require real-time or near-real-time synchronization for active components (e.g., interpretation and processing of incoming data) as referenced in [33]–[37]. However, no specific time scales (e.g., millisecond, second, or minute granularity) or service-level temporal requirements (e.g., 10ms latency for control loops) are explicitly defined. The integration of heterogeneous modeling tools (e.g., SysML, Simulink, or general-purpose languages like C++/Java) suggests that temporal granularity may vary depending on the abstraction layer (e.g., requirements, functional, or physical layers) as per MBSE methodologies like RFLP [18], [19]."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's orchestration and coordination are implicitly tied to the integration of multiple modeling notations and tools (e.g., SysML, Simulink, C++, or Java) as part of Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE). The architecture patterns are not explicitly detailed, but the context suggests that Digital Twins may act as active components to process data flows (unlike passive Digital Shadows) [35]–[37]. The use of architecture description languages (ADLs) like ACME [23], AADL [24], or MontiArc [25], [26] implies a component-based approach with defined interfaces, connectors, and composition rules. However, no specific orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices, or edge computing) or resource management strategies (e.g., load balancing, QoS policies) are mentioned. The coordination mechanisms are likely derived from the integration of these tools, but no concrete patterns (e.g., service-oriented, peer-to-peer) or system-wide governance models are described."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The lifecycle begins with **requirements modeling**, where system needs are formalized using structured methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** or **OOSEM (Object-Oriented Systems Engineering Methodology)**. This is followed by **functional architecture modeling**, where **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition relationships. The logical architecture layer further refines behavior using **state machines (SysML v1.7) or MontiArc Automaton**, while the product/technical layer integrates **simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java)** for detailed behavioral modeling.

Transition management between phases is facilitated by **heterogeneous modeling tools** and **interoperability frameworks**, ensuring seamless integration of models across abstraction layers. Lifecycle governance is implicitly supported by **MBSE frameworks**, which enforce structured modeling practices, version control (e.g., via tool-specific repositories), and iterative refinement cycles. The lifecycle also accommodates **evolution management** through modular updates to components or connectors in ADLs, enabling incremental refinement of the DT’s representation without full reengineering. No explicit mention of certification or regulatory compliance frameworks is provided in the document, but adherence to **industry-standard modeling languages (e.g., SysML, AADL)** ensures alignment with broader systems engineering practices.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity in the Digital Twin system is implicitly addressed through the Digital Shadow’s one-way data flow, which relies on contextual data traces for aggregation and abstraction. Validation is inferred from comparisons between simulated data (e.g., via SysML or Simulink) and measured data, ensuring behavioral consistency. Uncertainty quantification frameworks or explicit error correction mechanisms are not detailed in the provided documents. Quality assurance processes are not explicitly described beyond the reliance on MBSE methodologies and ADLs for system modeling."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly detail network infrastructure for digital twins, but it mentions **MQTT** as a communication protocol for sensor data streaming in the context of digital shadows. No specific network topology (e.g., edge-to-cloud), bandwidth/latency requirements, or security protocols (e.g., encryption standards) are provided. The reference to **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like SysML and AADL implies component-based connectivity but does not specify technical connection details beyond protocol usage."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems via data exchange protocols**, including **MQTT for streaming sensor data** (as referenced in the context of Digital Shadows and implied for active Digital Twins). The document also mentions **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**, a key **interoperability standard** (per the abstract), which facilitates semantic integration with enterprise systems or other Digital Twins by defining standardized data models and interfaces. However, the document does **not detail dependencies on external systems** like **ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) or MES (Manufacturing Execution Systems)**, nor does it specify integration patterns with other Digital Twins beyond the implied use of **AAS** for cross-system communication. While **OPC UA** is not explicitly mentioned, the reliance on **MQTT** and **AAS** suggests adherence to **industrial IoT (IIoT) standards** for horizontal integration. The lack of explicit mention of **API gateways, middleware, or cloud platforms** (e.g., AWS IoT, Azure Digital Twins) limits the description to **protocol-level and standard-based integration** rather than end-to-end system dependencies."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry standards** through the adoption of:
- **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implemented as per **industry 4.0 specifications** (e.g., **IEC 62424** alignment for asset modeling, though not explicitly cited).
- **Modeling Languages**: **SysML** (as per **OMG standards**) for systems engineering, with **AADL** for component-level specifications (no specific version or compliance level mentioned).
- **Data Protocols**: **MQTT** for sensor data streaming (standardized under **OASIS MQTT protocol**), though no specific version (e.g., MQTT 5.0) is referenced.
- **Simulation Tools**: **MathWorks Simulink** (aligned with **MODDELIO standards** for model exchange, though not explicitly named in the document).
No explicit **certification requirements** (e.g., ISO 23247 for Digital Twins) or **interoperability frameworks** (e.g., **OPC UA**) are mentioned beyond the general use of **AAS** and **MQTT**.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system referenced in the provided documents does not explicitly detail cybersecurity protocols, access control mechanisms, or threat mitigation strategies. However, the context of **RAMI 4.0** (as referenced in [48] and [49]) implies a structured approach to industrial cybersecurity, though no specific implementation details (e.g., encryption standards, authentication frameworks, or fail-safe mechanisms) are described in the documents. The **MABI robotic system** ([44]) is physically constrained within a **safety cell**, which inherently mitigates operational risks by isolating the robot from external hazards. No predictive safety monitoring, Digital Shadow-based fail-safe operations, or detailed risk assessment methodologies are mentioned in the provided documents."
]
